name: DNSP-11
subtitle: GDNF-Derived Peptide | Dopaminergic Neuroprotection

modifications: Derived from the pro-region of glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF)

overview: DNSP-11 is an 11-amino acid peptide derived from the pro-domain of glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF). It was designed to capture the neuroprotective properties of GDNF while avoiding the limitations of the full-length protein, including poor blood-brain barrier penetration, off-target effects, and manufacturing complexity. Preclinical research demonstrates that DNSP-11 protects and stimulates dopaminergic neurons, making it a compound of significant interest for Parkinson's disease research and biohacking communities focused on dopamine system health.
mechanism: DNSP-11 acts on dopaminergic neurons through mechanisms distinct from full-length GDNF. Rather than binding to the canonical GFRalpha1/RET receptor complex, DNSP-11 appears to engage alternative signaling pathways to promote dopaminergic neuron survival, stimulate dopamine release, and protect against neurotoxin-induced damage. It enhances tyrosine hydroxylase expression (the rate-limiting enzyme in dopamine synthesis) and supports mitochondrial function in dopaminergic neurons.
keyBenefits: Protects dopaminergic neurons from neurotoxic insults
Stimulates dopamine production via tyrosine hydroxylase upregulation
Smaller molecule with improved bioavailability compared to full GDNF
Does not require GFRalpha1/RET receptor complex for activity
Preclinical evidence for neuroprotection in Parkinson's disease models
Compatible with intranasal and subcutaneous administration

items.name: Dopaminergic Neuron Protection
items.description: Protects dopamine-producing neurons from neurotoxin-induced damage in preclinical Parkinson's disease models.

items.name: Tyrosine Hydroxylase Support
items.description: Upregulates tyrosine hydroxylase expression, supporting the rate-limiting step in dopamine biosynthesis.

items.name: Mitochondrial Function
items.description: Supports mitochondrial health in dopaminergic neurons, which is often compromised in neurodegenerative conditions.

items.name: Motivation and Drive
items.description: Biohacker reports suggest improvements in baseline motivation and drive, consistent with dopaminergic system support.

items.name: Mood Baseline
items.description: Anecdotal reports of improved mood stability and emotional baseline, likely mediated through dopamine pathway optimization.

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal administration allows for potential direct CNS delivery via olfactory transport, bypassing the blood-brain barrier. This is a popular route in the biohacking community.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Calculate concentration per spray based on desired dose

keyBenefits: Potential direct brain delivery via olfactory transport

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection provides reliable and consistent dosing. This route relies on systemic absorption and blood-brain barrier penetration.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Clean vial top with alcohol swab
Inject bacteriostatic water slowly down the side of the vial
Gently swirl until completely dissolved (do not shake)
Draw desired dose into insulin syringe
Inject subcutaneously into abdominal fat or thigh
Refrigerate reconstituted solution; use within 4 weeks

interactions.notes: Complementary neurotrophic pathways; Semax acts via BDNF upregulation while DNSP-11 targets dopaminergic neuroprotection through GDNF-related mechanisms.
Both support dopaminergic function through different mechanisms. Bromantane enhances tyrosine hydroxylase gene expression while DNSP-11 provides neurotrophic support.
Both target dopaminergic neuron health. 9-Me-BC promotes dendrite growth and dopamine synthesis; DNSP-11 provides neurotrophic protection.

timeline.effects: Subtle changes may begin; most users report minimal noticeable effects during the initial period
Anecdotal reports of gradual improvement in baseline mood, motivation, and sense of drive
Potential peak of subjective benefits; improved emotional resilience and cognitive motivation reported by some users
Limited data on offset timeline; neurotrophic effects may persist beyond active dosing period

common: Nasal irritation or mild burning sensation (intranasal route)
Injection site reactions such as redness or swelling (subcutaneous route)
stopSigns: Severe or persistent nasal irritation or bleeding
Signs of allergic reaction (rash, swelling, difficulty breathing)
contraindications: Very limited human safety data; use at own risk
Consult a physician before combining with dopaminergic medications
monitoring: Start with the lowest dose (100mcg) to assess individual response
Monitor for changes in mood, sleep, or motivation
Track injection site or nasal reactions
Avoid combining with high-dose dopaminergic drugs without medical supervision

bad: Cloudy or discolored solution after reconstitution
Powder that fails to dissolve completely

references.title: DNSP-11 Is a Novel GDNF Pro-Peptide That Protects Dopaminergic Neurons in a Rat Model of Parkinson's Disease
references.authors: Bradley LH, Fuqua J, Richardson A, et al.
references.keyFindings: DNSP-11 protected dopaminergic neurons in a 6-OHDA rat model of Parkinson's disease, preserving tyrosine hydroxylase-positive neurons and improving motor behavior without the side effects associated with full-length GDNF.

references.title: Dopamine Neuron Stimulating Peptide-11 (DNSP-11): A Novel, Small Peptide That Provides Long-Term Protection in a Rat Model of Parkinson's Disease
references.authors: Kelps KA, Turchan-Cholewo J, Bhatt I, et al.
references.keyFindings: A single injection of DNSP-11 provided sustained neuroprotection of dopaminergic neurons over multiple weeks in a 6-OHDA Parkinson's model, demonstrating long-lasting neurotrophic effects from the GDNF pro-domain peptide.

references.title: DNSP-11 Induces Behavioral Recovery and Nigrostriatal Neurochemical Changes in a Unilateral 6-OHDA Rat Model of Parkinson's Disease
references.authors: Fuqua JL, Littrell OM, Lundblad M, et al.
references.keyFindings: DNSP-11 improved motor behavior and restored striatal dopamine neurochemistry in a unilateral 6-OHDA lesion model, supporting its potential as a targeted dopaminergic neuroprotective agent.

references.title: Identification of Two Novel Peptides from the GDNF Pro-Domain That Bind to GFRalpha1
references.authors: Bradley LH, Fuqua JL, Bhatt I, et al.
references.keyFindings: Identified DNSP-11 and a related peptide from the GDNF pro-domain with binding affinity to GFRalpha1, establishing a potential receptor-level mechanism for dopaminergic neuroprotective effects.

latestResearch.title: DNSP-11 Induces Behavioral Recovery and Nigrostriatal Neurochemical Changes
latestResearch.summary: Extended study confirming DNSP-11 restores dopamine-related neurochemistry and behavioral function in Parkinson's disease rat models, reinforcing preclinical evidence for translational potential.

quickStats.cycleDuration: 4-6 weeks on, followed by a break
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: -20C long-term; Reconstituted: 2-8C, use within 4 weeks
